title: FastSAM-3D: Real-Time 3D Segment Anything with Sparse Point Prompts
summary: The Segment Anything Model (SAM) has demonstrated remarkable zero-shot segmentation capabilities in 2D images. However, extending SAM to 3D point clouds remains challenging due to the irregular structure and large scale of 3D data. In this paper, we propose FastSAM-3D, a real-time 3D segmentation framework that efficiently processes point cloud data with sparse point prompts. Our method introduces a lightweight 3D encoder based on sparse convolutions and a novel prompt-guided attention mechanism that enables efficient interaction between point prompts and 3D features. We further propose a multi-scale feature aggregation module that captures both local geometric details and global semantic context. Extensive experiments on ScanNet, S3DIS, and nuScenes datasets demonstrate that FastSAM-3D achieves state-of-the-art performance while running at 15 FPS on a single GPU, which is 5x faster than existing methods. Code is available at https://github.com/zhangwei/FastSAM-3D.

title: LongRAG: Extending Retrieval-Augmented Generation to Million-Token Contexts
summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become a standard approach for grounding large language models in external knowledge. However, existing RAG systems struggle with extremely long contexts, often failing to retrieve and synthesize information spread across hundreds of documents. We present LongRAG, a novel framework that extends RAG capabilities to handle million-token contexts through three key innovations: (1) a hierarchical retrieval mechanism that operates at document, paragraph, and sentence levels; (2) a progressive context compression technique that preserves salient information while reducing token count by 10x; and (3) a retrieval-aware attention mechanism that dynamically adjusts the model's focus based on retrieval confidence scores. Evaluated on multi-document QA benchmarks including MuSiQue, HotpotQA, and our newly introduced LongBench-1M dataset, LongRAG achieves 23% improvement in answer accuracy over existing RAG baselines while maintaining comparable latency.

title: ReasonAgent: Teaching Language Models to Reason Through Self-Play Reinforcement Learning
summary: While large language models have shown impressive capabilities in various tasks, their reasoning abilities remain limited, particularly in multi-step mathematical and logical problems. We introduce ReasonAgent, a framework that enhances LLM reasoning through self-play reinforcement learning. Our approach trains a reasoning policy network that learns to decompose complex problems into manageable sub-steps, verify intermediate results, and backtrack when errors are detected. The key innovation is a dual-agent self-play mechanism where a proposer agent generates reasoning chains and a critic agent evaluates them, with both agents improving through iterative competition. We also introduce a novel reward shaping technique that provides dense feedback at each reasoning step rather than only at the final answer. Experiments on GSM8K, MATH, and ARC-Challenge show that ReasonAgent improves reasoning accuracy by 18-31% over chain-of-thought prompting baselines, with particularly strong gains on problems requiring 5+ reasoning steps.

title: DiffEdit-3D: Text-Guided 3D Scene Editing via Score Distillation
summary: Text-guided 3D scene editing has emerged as a promising direction for intuitive content creation. However, existing methods either produce inconsistent edits across viewpoints or require expensive per-scene optimization. We propose DiffEdit-3D, a framework for consistent and efficient text-guided 3D scene editing. Our method leverages a pre-trained 2D diffusion model and introduces a novel multi-view score distillation sampling (MV-SDS) loss that ensures geometric and appearance consistency across all viewpoints simultaneously. We further propose an attention-based edit localization module that automatically identifies and modifies only the relevant regions of the 3D scene, preserving unedited areas. A progressive editing strategy is employed to handle complex edits by breaking them into simpler sequential modifications. Experiments on both synthetic and real-world 3D scenes demonstrate that DiffEdit-3D produces high-quality, view-consistent edits while being 3x faster than optimization-based alternatives.

title: CodeBridge: Bridging Natural Language and Code Through Bidirectional Translation
summary: The gap between natural language descriptions and executable code remains a significant barrier in software development. We present CodeBridge, a unified model for bidirectional translation between natural language and code across multiple programming languages. Unlike existing code generation models that focus primarily on natural language to code translation, CodeBridge jointly trains on both directions, enabling it to generate code from descriptions and produce accurate natural language explanations of code snippets. Our architecture introduces a shared semantic space where both natural language and code representations are aligned through contrastive learning. We also propose a syntax-aware decoding strategy that leverages grammar constraints to ensure generated code is always syntactically valid. Trained on a curated dataset of 2M aligned code-description pairs spanning Python, Java, JavaScript, and C++, CodeBridge achieves new state-of-the-art results on HumanEval (pass@1: 89.2%), MBPP (pass@1: 82.7%), and CodeXGLUE code summarization benchmarks.
